query_normalized: quantifying the influence of initial conditions on chaotic systems physics
rationale: This paper satisfies acceptance criteria (a) and (d) because it directly measures the sensitivity of chaotic systems (specifically the Lorenz attractor) to infinitesimal perturbations of initial conditions and parameters, which is the core mechanism of the user's question, and it provides the foundational methodology (forward and adjoint sensitivity algorithms) for quantifying this influence.
